original_hypothesis.title: Airdrop claim window can be permanently disabled or made inconsistent due to missing sanity checks in MerkleClaimable.setConfig
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: MerkleClaimable.setConfig and _setConfig do not validate that claimStart < claimEnd, or that merkleRoot is non-zero when enabling claims. Misconfiguration (or malicious owner action) can set invalid parameters (e.g., claimStart > claimEnd or merkleRoot=0) causing ongoingClaim to revert for all claim attempts, effectively locking distribution until reconfigured.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a practical operational defect that can halt airdrop distribution and generate user-facing incidents. It is especially relevant because DeployERC20Airdrop initializes with zeros for claimStart/claimEnd/root, relying on a later setConfig call.
deployment_assumptions: Owner might accidentally set invalid claimStart/claimEnd or clear merkleRoot during an active distribution
No external safety checks (timelock/process) prevent such changes
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: DeployERC20Airdrop.s.sol initializes ERC20Airdrop with (claimStart=0, claimEnd=0, merkleRoot=0) implying later setConfig is required; MerkleClaimable._setConfig has no validation.

proof_of_signal.lines: _setConfig sets claimStart/claimEnd/merkleRoot directly; ongoingClaim reverts if any are 0 or time bounds invalid

preconditions: Owner calls setConfig with invalid parameters (accidental or malicious)
Users rely on on-chain claim path without alternative distribution mechanism
No upgrade/emergency override process immediately available

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Contract is deployed in a disabled state by script (0,0,0 root) and must be configured correctly later; a single bad setConfig halts all claims.

operational_questions: Is setConfig controlled by a timelock or multisig in production deployments?
Are there monitoring/alerts preventing claim window misconfiguration?
Is there an emergency migration plan if claim parameters are set incorrectly?
validation_requirements: Review ownership of airdrop proxies on target networks
Simulate misconfiguration scenarios and recovery steps
Check whether frontend/backend can detect and prevent invalid config writes

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Observe owner-controlled setConfig transactions and resulting claim behavior.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: If owner action sets invalid window/root, claims revert for all users.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Sustain incident by repeatedly toggling config (requires owner compromise/collusion).
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Requires owner error or compromise (low realism)

potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Low to Medium (owner can fix if available; otherwise upgrade required)

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: setConfig calls setting merkleRoot=0 or claimStart>=claimEnd

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
